diagrams.title: Chomsky Hierarchy and Decidability
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between language classes and their decidability properties
diagrams.purpose: Visualize which language classes have decidable problems vs undecidable

diagrams.title: Halting Problem Proof Flowchart
diagrams.description: Diagonalization proof showing contradiction when assuming halting problem is decidable
elements: Build D(P): if H(P,P) halts then loop else halt
If H says halts → D loops (contradiction)
If H says loops → D halts (contradiction)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the diagonalization proof technique for undecidability

diagrams.title: RE vs Recursive Venn Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the inclusion relationships between language classes
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the difference between decidable, semi-decidable, and non-RE languages

diagrams.title: Reduction Chain for Undecidability
diagrams.description: Shows how known undecidable problems can be reduced to prove other problems undecidable
elements: PCP also reduces to many problems
diagrams.purpose: Understand how reduction proves undecidability of new problems

visuals.title: Problems That Computers Cannot Solve
visuals.description: Visual diagram for problems that computers cannot solve
